core_mechanism: constituent (parton-like) decomposition of an extended, fast-moving coherent object into a distribution of on-shell quanta, with the hard output of a collision computed as incoherent pairwise on-shell scattering among those constituents in the fast-passage/eikonal limit, rather than as decay of the coherent background
brief_reason: The abstract explicitly proposes a formalism 'analogous to the partonic description of high-energy collisions' in which the colliding objects undergo nearly free passage and hard production 'arises from on-shell scatterings among the quanta constituting' them, replacing the coherent off-shell-background treatment.
